assumptions: A browser can reach the public Siemens Xcelerator Developer Portal.
The developer must choose among product families and developer intents before an executable onboarding route exists.

For the Building X Accounts API candidate, the developer has a working email address, can receive an email OTP, and has a Linux or macOS shell with curl. Windows requires different environment-variable syntax.
No customer account or connected building is assumed for the candidate, so the documented sandbox branch is used.

developer_goal: Use Siemens developer technology, with the exact product and API not yet chosen.

first_success.outcome: The portal gets a developer to product and API selection, but each Siemens product defines its own access model and result. A complete Building X Accounts API sandbox candidate reaches a successful `GET me/usergroups` response listing the UserGroups accessible to the machine user.
first_success.observable_signal: The response returns the UserGroups available to the machine user. For each returned group, the guide identifies roles in `relationships.authorizedAs`, accessible partitions in `relationships.hasAccessTo`, and the owning customer in `relationships.ownedBy`; it says listed partitions can be used with other APIs.

route_selection.surface: Building X Accounts API sandbox, first UserGroups response
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Building X Accounts API sandbox, first UserGroups response
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Siemens Xcelerator Marketplace and API World
candidates.reason: The official Xcelerator guide positions Marketplace and API World for exploring business value and placing orders, while the Developer Portal is the technical documentation surface.

candidates.name: Siemens Xcelerator Community
candidates.reason: The official guide positions the community as a collaboration and discussion hub rather than an executable development route.

candidates.name: Insights Hub developer documentation
candidates.reason: Insights Hub is one product family among many and is not selected as the Siemens-wide default.

required_path.action: Complete Siemens account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Siemens account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Siemens candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The response returns the UserGroups available to the machine user. For each returned group, the guide identifies roles in `relationships.authorizedAs`, accessible partitions in `relationships.hasAccessTo`, and the owning customer in `relationships.ownedBy`; it says listed partitions can be used with

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'other', 'requirement': 'A human must select a Siemens product or API family because the portal offers many peers and no Siemens-wide default implementation route.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S2', 'S3']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'identity', 'requirement': 'For the Building X sandbox candidate, a SiemensID activated through a working email address and email OTP verification.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S4', 'S5']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'access', 'requirement': 'Building X sandbox access requested through the Developer Dashboard; credentials are documented as granted immediately and visible on My Credentials.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S4']}

external_gates.description: The portal offers four peer intent routes and many product families with no default platform-wide path.
The Building X candidate requires SiemensID, but SiemensID itself does not authorize a Siemens service.
SiemensID self-registration requires four identity fields and a password meeting length, repetition, and character-class rules.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: In the selected Building X Accounts API sandbox route, first success is demonstrated when GET me/usergroups returns the UserGroups accessible to the machine user.

excluded.item: Selecting a customer ID and calling List Partitions for customer to retrieve partition and company names.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Using discovered partitions with Building Operations or another Building X API.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Requesting later pages of a paginated UserGroups response unless required to observe any result.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Purchasing or subscribing through Siemens Xcelerator Marketplace or API World.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: Which Siemens product or API should define the platform-wide first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes every later prerequisite, account, entitlement, credential, software, action, and success signal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The Developer Portal presents many products and four peer intents; the Xcelerator overview does not select a technical default.

uncertainties.question: What fields, confirmations, and policy gates appear in the authenticated Building X sandbox request?
uncertainties.impact: Additional atomic actions may exist between SiemensID login and immediate credential issuance.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The public guide states Request access and immediate credentials, but the request UI is session-gated and its fields are not enumerated.

uncertainties.question: Which identity fields and email-verification steps remain when a new user registers directly with a SiemensID passkey?
uncertainties.impact: The direct-passkey route may replace more than the password action and would change the exact account-creation sequence.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The current SiemensID FAQ says new users can register directly with a passkey but does not reconstruct the full registration sequence for that branch.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Siemens show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
